serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of wheat bran is approximately 2 tablespoons (15 g), commonly added to cereals, smoothies, yogurt, or baked goods to boost dietary fibre intake.

nutrient_source.title: Wheat bran, crude — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 20077)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for wheat bran, crude (FDC ID 20077) used as primary nutrient anchor. Singapore HPB input values cross-referenced and are consistent with USDA data. HPB values retained for energy (267 kcal), protein (14.8 g), fat (4.1 g), carbohydrate (22.6 g), sugar (2.7 g), sodium (6 mg), dietary fibre (41.8 g), potassium (1200 mg), calcium (75 mg), iron (11 mg), cholesterol (0 mg). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and micronutrient detail sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 20077.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Wheat bran GI of approximately 30 (low) is documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables of GI and GL (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). GL per 15 g serving calculated as: (30 × 22.6 × 0.15) / 100 = 1.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (6 mg/100 g) and exceptionally high potassium (1200 mg/100 g) with high dietary fibre supporting DASH diet targets
hypertension.guidance: Wheat bran is highly suitable for hypertension management; its negligible sodium content, exceptional potassium-to-sodium ratio, and very high soluble fibre (41.8 g/100 g) collectively support blood pressure reduction consistent with DASH dietary guidelines — introduce gradually (1–2 tbsp/day) to minimise gastrointestinal discomfort.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Exceptionally high dietary fibre (41.8 g/100 g) slows gastric emptying and glucose absorption; low GI of 30 and low sugar (2.7 g/100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Wheat bran is one of the most diabetes-appropriate grain products available; its low glycemic index (GI 30), very high dietary fibre, and low sugar content significantly attenuate postprandial glucose response — a typical 15 g serving contributes a glycemic load of only 1.0, making it safe for daily use in Type 2 Diabetes meal plans when consumed in appropriate portions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High insoluble and soluble dietary fibre (41.8 g/100 g) reduces LDL cholesterol; zero cholesterol; low saturated fat (0.6 g/100 g); high potassium and magnesium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Wheat bran is cardioprotective: its very high dietary fibre content is associated with significant LDL cholesterol reduction, its zero cholesterol and low saturated fat profile pose no cardiovascular risk, and its high potassium (1200 mg/100 g) and magnesium (611 mg/100 g) support vascular health — regular inclusion at 15–30 g/day is consistent with AHA dietary fibre recommendations for cardiovascular risk reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High dietary fibre supports gut microbiome diversity and immune modulation; negligible saturated fat; however, gluten content is a concern for RA patients with coeliac disease
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Wheat bran may benefit RA patients through its high dietary fibre content supporting gut microbiome diversity and immune regulation, but patients with confirmed gluten sensitivity or coeliac disease should avoid wheat bran and consult a dietitian for alternative high-fibre options.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Wheat bran is the hard outer layer of the wheat kernel, separated from the endosperm and germ during the milling of whole wheat into white flour. It appears as light brown, coarse flakes with a dry, nutty flavour and slightly rough texture. Nutritionally, wheat bran is one of the most fibre-dense foods available, providing an extraordinary 41.8 g of dietary fibre per 100 g — primarily insoluble fibre — which promotes bowel regularity, slows gastric emptying, and attenuates postprandial glucose absorption. It is also a significant source of plant protein (14.8 g/100 g), B-vitamins including niacin (13.6 mg), vitamin B6 (1.30 mg), and thiamine (0.52 mg), as well as minerals including iron (11 mg), potassium (1200 mg), magnesium (611 mg), phosphorus (1013 mg), and zinc (7.27 mg) per 100 g. Its glycemic index is low (GI ≈ 30), making it suitable for blood glucose management. Wheat bran is widely used as a dietary supplement added to breakfast cereals, smoothies, yogurt, muffins, bread, and other baked goods. It is plant-based, vegan, and halal, but contains gluten and is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or wheat allergy. Clinically, wheat bran is recommended for Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease management due to its fibre, potassium, and low sodium profile.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from Singapore HPB database (input, confidence 0.95) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 20077) for wheat bran, crude. Both sources are approved primary sources. Values are consistent across sources. GI sourced from University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are fully supported.
notes: Cholesterol is confirmed zero (0.0 mg/100 g) per both HPB and USDA SR Legacy — wheat bran is a plant-derived product and inherently contains no cholesterol. Trans_fat_g set to 0.0 as wheat bran contains negligible trans fats per USDA SR Legacy. Vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, and vitamin_d_mcg set to 0.0 as wheat bran contains none of these nutrients per USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 20077. Saturated fat (0.6 g), monounsaturated fat (0.6 g), polyunsaturated fat (2.2 g), and all micronutrient values not present in the HPB input are sourced from USDA SR Legacy FDC ID 20077. Potassium (1200 mg), calcium (75 mg), and iron (11 mg) are retained from HPB input and are consistent with USDA data. Wheat bran contains gluten and is not suitable for individuals with coeliac disease or wheat allergy. The rheumatoid arthritis health context caution regarding gluten sensitivity is clinically important. Ontology codes: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 and SNOMED CT 412152003 are cited as defensible matches; FoodEx2 code A00LC is cited as the closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor for wheat bran — reviewers should verify FoodEx2 code currency against the EFSA FoodEx2 browser.
